context: Evaluates the implementation of the attachment save Tauri command, which encrypts binary attachment data using a freshly generated per-attachment DEK and stores both encrypted metadata and encrypted binary data. The implementation must compute a SHA-256 integrity hash of the original unencrypted data, encrypt the DEK with the master key, and write two separate files to the vault directory.

checklist.name: SHA-256 hash computation
checklist.description: Computes the SHA-256 digest of the original (unencrypted) input bytes using the `sha2` crate and stores the result as a hex-encoded string in `Attachment.sha256`.

checklist.name: Per-attachment DEK generation
checklist.description: Calls `generate_dek()` to produce a unique random 32-byte DEK for each attachment, then encrypts the DEK with the vault master key and stores the DEK ciphertext and nonce in the metadata.

checklist.name: Binary data encryption
checklist.description: Encrypts the raw attachment bytes with the per-attachment DEK using AES-256-GCM (`encrypt()`), writing only the ciphertext bytes to `attachments/{uuid}.bin`.

checklist.name: Dual-file persistence
checklist.description: Writes the encrypted metadata (JSON with DEK ciphertext, nonce, filename, MIME, size, SHA-256) to `attachments/{uuid}.json` and the encrypted binary data to `attachments/{uuid}.bin`.

checklist.name: Locked vault error
checklist.description: Returns a `String` error when `AppState.master_key` is `None` (vault is locked) rather than panicking or proceeding with encryption.

checklist.name: Attachment return value
checklist.description: Returns an `Attachment` struct with the new UUID, `note_id`, `filename`, `mime`, `size` (byte count of original data), and `sha256` fields correctly populated.
